(born October 15, 1966) is an American [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") singer-songwriter and musician. He has been nominated for four [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award").

**Eric Benét Jordan** (born October 15, 1966) is an American [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") singer-songwriter and musician. He has been nominated for four [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award").

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eric_Ben%C3%A9t&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Benét was born on October 15, 1966, in [Mobile, Alabama](/wiki/Mobile,_Alabama "Mobile, Alabama"). He was raised in [Milwaukee, Wisconsin](/wiki/Milwaukee,_Wisconsin "Milwaukee, Wisconsin"), the youngest of five siblings.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Benét expressed an interest in music at an early age, but was initially encouraged by his father, a police officer who died of cancer, to attend college and pursue a "stable" career.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He graduated from [Milwaukee Trade and Technical High School](/wiki/Lynde_%26_Harry_Bradley_Technology_and_Trade_School "Lynde & Harry Bradley Technology and Trade School") and enrolled in college, but later dropped out.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eric_Ben%C3%A9t&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Benét began his career as a member of a group called Gerard in the late 1980s. Later, Benét, his sister Lisa, and his cousin George Nash Jr. formed a band called Benét and released a self-titled album in 1992 which sold over 100,000 copies. In 1994, he signed with [Warner Bros. Records](/wiki/Warner_Bros._Records "Warner Bros. Records"), releasing his solo debut album, *[True to Myself](/wiki/True_to_Myself "True to Myself")* in 1996. Individual songs from the album were successful, including the top-ten R&B hits "Spiritual Thang", "Femininity" and "Let's Stay Together", which originally appeared on the soundtrack of the film *[A Thin Line Between Love and Hate](/wiki/A_Thin_Line_Between_Love_and_Hate "A Thin Line Between Love and Hate")*. His second album, *A Day in the Life*, was released in 1999 and featured his smash hit "[Spend My Life With You](/wiki/Spend_My_Life_With_You "Spend My Life With You") (featuring Tamia)". The song rose to No. 1 on the US Billboard R&B charts (for 3 weeks), was certified gold, and nominated for a 2000 Grammy Award for "Best R&B Performance by a Duo or Group". The album also won a Soul Train Music Award for "Best R&B/Soul Album, Male".

In between albums, Benét collaborated with his then-labelmates [Somethin' for the People](/wiki/Somethin%27_for_the_People "Somethin' for the People") on their 1997 album *This Time It's Personal* singing leads on the single "Act Like You Want It". In 2001, he recorded a song for [Earth, Wind, and Fire](/wiki/Earth,_Wind,_and_Fire "Earth, Wind, and Fire")'s 30th anniversary CD, and began work on a new album titled *[Better and Better](/wiki/Better_and_Better_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "Better and Better (Eric Benét album)")*, which was set to be a departure from his previous work. Benét's label, however, refused to release this album, forcing him to stay in the R&B genre. Due to disagreements regarding music style and creative freedom, Benét left Warner Bros. and signed to [Reprise](/wiki/Reprise_Records "Reprise Records")-distributed label [Friday Records](/wiki/Tim_Blixseth "Tim Blixseth") and recorded his next album, *[Hurricane](/wiki/Hurricane_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "Hurricane (Eric Benét album)"),*[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) which became his third studio album following its release on June 21, 2005, in the US. "[I Wanna Be Loved](/wiki/I_Wanna_Be_Loved "I Wanna Be Loved")" was the song that received the most airplay. The single reached number two on the Urban Adult Contemporary chart.

His fourth album *[Love & Life](/wiki/Love_%26_Life_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "Love & Life (Eric Benét album)")* was released on September 9, 2008. It debuted at number eleven on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") and number two on the [Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums](/wiki/Top_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Albums "Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums") chart, making this his own highest peak position since *[A Day in the Life](/wiki/A_Day_in_the_Life_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "A Day in the Life (Eric Benét album)")* in 1999.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The first single "You're the Only One" is also his first top 20 hit on the R&B chart in two years. *[Lost in Time](/wiki/Lost_in_Time_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "Lost in Time (Eric Benét album)")* was Benét's fifth studio album which was released in the US on November 30, 2010. The first single "Sometimes I Cry" reached number one on the Hot Adult R&B Songs Chart. With its musical direction representing Benét's homage to the sweet soul sound of the early to mid-1970s, the album featured duets with [Faith Evans](/wiki/Faith_Evans "Faith Evans"); [Chrisette Michelle](/wiki/Chrisette_Michelle "Chrisette Michelle"); [Ledisi](/wiki/Ledisi "Ledisi"); and [Eddie Levert](/wiki/Eddie_Levert "Eddie Levert") of [The O'Jays](/wiki/The_O%27Jays "The O'Jays").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

On June 5, 2012, Benét released his album *The One*. It was the first album released on Benét's newly formed record label Jordan House Records, which he created in partnership with [EMI](/wiki/EMI "EMI"). "Real Love" was the first single off *The One* and was the No. 1 added song to Urban AC when shipped to radio in October 2011. In 2013, Eric Benét's sixth studio album, *The One*, won him the SoulTracks Readers' Choice Award for Best Male Vocalist. *The One* delivered 3 big hit tracks including 'Runnin,' 'Real Love,' and 'Harriett Jones.'[[7]](#cite_note-7) In April 2014, Benét signed R&B singer-songwriter [Calvin Richardson](/wiki/Calvin_Richardson "Calvin Richardson") and [Goapele](/wiki/Goapele "Goapele") to his imprint along with [BMG](/wiki/BMG_Rights_Management "BMG Rights Management")/[Primary Wave Music](/wiki/Primary_Wave_Music "Primary Wave Music") to release their upcoming albums in the US.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 2014, Benét also released an international album, *The Other One*, teaming up with the European production collective, The Afropeans to revisit his 2012 album *The One*.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Later that year, he released an album of classic cover songs, exclusively distributed in Japan, titled, *From E to U, Vol. 1*.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) In April 2016, he announced a new single "Sunshine" set to premiere on May 13, ahead of his album that was due in the fall.[[12]](#cite_note-12) On October 7, 2016, Benét released [his eponymous eighth studio album](/wiki/Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_(album) "Eric Benét (album)") and first major album release since 2012's "[The One](/wiki/The_One_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "The One (Eric Benét album)")", the album featured guest appearances from [Tamia](/wiki/Tamia "Tamia"), [Arturo Sandoval](/wiki/Arturo_Sandoval "Arturo Sandoval") and [MC Lyte](/wiki/MC_Lyte "MC Lyte"). In 2020, during the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") Benet started an on demand powdered by Omnis titled *Eric After Dark* (stylized as ***Eric @fter Dark***).

On April 25, 2025, "Can't Wait", a song in collaboration with [Keri Hilson](/wiki/Keri_Hilson "Keri Hilson") was released.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

### Acting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eric_Ben%C3%A9t&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Acting")]

Benét has also been active as an actor. He had a recurring role on *[For Your Love](/wiki/For_Your_Love_(TV_series) "For Your Love (TV series)")* (1998–2002). In 2001, he made his big-screen debut—alongside that of singer [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey")—in *[Glitter](/wiki/Glitter_(film) "Glitter (film)")*; the film (which has elements of romance, drama, and comedy) was loosely based on Carey's own life and early experiences within the music industry. Additionally, in 2005, he appeared on *[Half & Half](/wiki/Half_%26_Half "Half & Half")*, in which he portrayed Reece Wilcox, and had a recurring role on the 2007 [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV") show *Kaya*. On July 9, 2011, Benét's second feature film role, *Trinity Goodheart*, premiered at the [American Black Film Festival](/wiki/American_Black_Film_Festival "American Black Film Festival"). It aired on [GMC](/wiki/Up_TV "Up TV") on August 20.[[15]](#cite_note-15) In 2013, Benét appeared on the second season of [BET](/wiki/BET "BET")'s hit television show, the *[Real Husbands of Hollywood](/wiki/Real_Husbands_of_Hollywood "Real Husbands of Hollywood")*. He was a guest star, alongside [Kevin Hart](/wiki/Kevin_Hart_(actor) "Kevin Hart (actor)"), [Bobby Brown](/wiki/Bobby_Brown "Bobby Brown"), and [Boris Kodjoe](/wiki/Boris_Kodjoe "Boris Kodjoe"). In 2022, he appeared in *[Greed: A Seven Deadly Sins Story](/wiki/Seven_Deadly_Sins_(film_series) "Seven Deadly Sins (film series)")*.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eric_Ben%C3%A9t&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Benét's eldest daughter India Brianne Jordan was born on December 3, 1991, to his partner Tami Stauff, who died in a car accident on April 24, 1993.[[16]](#cite_note-16) He married actress [Halle Berry](/wiki/Halle_Berry "Halle Berry") in January 2001, but by early October 2003, they had separated,[[17]](#cite_note-Peopleseparation-17) with the divorce finalized in January 2005.[[18]](#cite_note-Peopledivorced-18)

On July 31, 2011, Benét married Toronto native Manuela Testolini, ex-wife of [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)"). Their first child together, a girl named Lucia Bella, was born on December 21, 2011.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Their second daughter, Amoura Luna, was born on July 13, 2014.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eric_Ben%C3%A9t&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Discography")]

Main article: [Eric Benét discography](/wiki/Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_discography "Eric Benét discography")

**Studio albums**

* *[True to Myself](/wiki/True_to_Myself "True to Myself")* (1996)
* *[A Day in the Life](/wiki/A_Day_in_the_Life_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "A Day in the Life (Eric Benét album)")* (1999)
* *[Hurricane](/wiki/Hurricane_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "Hurricane (Eric Benét album)")* (2005)
* *[Love & Life](/wiki/Love_%26_Life_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "Love & Life (Eric Benét album)")* (2008)
* *[Lost in Time](/wiki/Lost_in_Time_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "Lost in Time (Eric Benét album)")* (2010)
* *[The One](/wiki/The_One_(Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_album) "The One (Eric Benét album)")* (2012)
* *[From E to U: Volume 1](/wiki/From_E_to_U:_Volume_1 "From E to U: Volume 1")* (2014)
* *[Eric Benét](/wiki/Eric_Ben%C3%A9t_(album) "Eric Benét (album)")* (2016)
* *[The Co-Star](/wiki/The_Co-Star "The Co-Star")* (2025)

Collaboration albums

* *Benét* (with Benét) (1992)
